All foundation tasks complete; scaffold builds and the trait boundaries are in place.

Toolchain pinned + documented (docs/TOOLING.md, requirements.txt), tool discovery in core::tools, 'lob doctor' preflight implemented and verified; RC low-pass SKiDL circuit emits a clean netlist end-to-end.

Toolchain pinned + documented (docs/TOOLING.md, requirements.txt), tool discovery in core::tools, 'lob doctor' preflight implemented and verified; RC low-pass SKiDL circuit emits a clean netlist end-to-end.

SKiDL runner (core::skidl::SkidlRunner) shells out via the venv python, runs in a scratch work_dir so artifacts stay out of the repo, captures netlist + ERC report, surfaces missing-interpreter/non-zero-exit as StageError (not panic). Wired as stage 1 of 'lob run'; verified end-to-end on examples/rc_lowpass.py. 10 unit tests, fmt+clippy clean.

core::netlist parses the KiCad S-expr netlist into the Circuit model (first CircuitSource producer): parts (refdes/value/footprint/libsource) + nets (name + pins), deterministically sorted. Self-contained S-expr reader, no new deps. Wired as stage 2 of 'lob run' (parses the real generated netlist: 2 parts, 3 nets). 14 unit tests incl. round-trip on an RC netlist fixture; fmt+clippy clean.

core::spice generates a SPICE deck from the Circuit (pin→net inversion, GND→0, R/C/L support, IN/OUT/GND harness), runs 'ngspice -b', and parses the AC response (freq, vdb). AcResult exposes passband gain + interpolated -3dB cutoff. Verified: 601-point sweep on the RC filter. 9 spice/units tests.

core::verify::check_rc_cutoff computes analytic 1/(2piRC) from parsed R/C values and asserts the simulated -3dB cutoff within a configurable tolerance. End-to-end: expected 1000.97 Hz vs simulated 1000.96 Hz (0.001% err). Failure path unit-tested (wrong cutoff → failed outcome w/ diff). Non-single-RC → skipped.

Completed the 6-stage Phase 0 loop. validate (core::validate): surfaces ERC WARNING/ERROR lines as structured findings, fails only on ERC errors. bom (core::bom): groups parts by value+footprint, qty+refdes, CSV + aligned table, flags missing footprints. Wired into 'lob run' in DESIGN order skidl→parse→validate→simulate→verify→bom with per-stage report + non-zero exit. Verified end-to-end on RC filter: all 6 stages pass, exit 0, BOM CSV written. 30 core tests, fmt+clippy clean.

Completed the 6-stage Phase 0 loop. validate (core::validate): surfaces ERC WARNING/ERROR lines as structured findings, fails only on ERC errors. bom (core::bom): groups parts by value+footprint, qty+refdes, CSV + aligned table, flags missing footprints. Wired into 'lob run' in DESIGN order skidl→parse→validate→simulate→verify→bom with per-stage report + non-zero exit. Verified end-to-end on RC filter: all 6 stages pass, exit 0, BOM CSV written. 30 core tests, fmt+clippy clean.

Completed the 6-stage Phase 0 loop. validate (core::validate): surfaces ERC WARNING/ERROR lines as structured findings, fails only on ERC errors. bom (core::bom): groups parts by value+footprint, qty+refdes, CSV + aligned table, flags missing footprints. Wired into 'lob run' in DESIGN order skidl→parse→validate→simulate→verify→bom with per-stage report + non-zero exit. Verified end-to-end on RC filter: all 6 stages pass, exit 0, BOM CSV written. 30 core tests, fmt+clippy clean.

Implemented: components carry their SPICE model, resolved FROM the component (core::symbols reads the KiCad symbol's Sim.Device/Name/Library/Pins — which SKiDL's netlist drops — and the .sp subckt terminal order). core::spice::SpiceModel + generic generate_ac_deck instantiates subckt (X-line + .include) or primitive (R/C/L) with supply rails, ZERO per-device conditionals. Verified: RC via primitive path, op-amp via the real kicad_builtin_opamp subckt, both through the same generic path. S-expr reader promoted to shared core::sexpr.

Non-inverting op-amp gain stage (examples/opamp_noninv.py, Rf=9k/Rg=1k -> gain 10) passes the full 6-stage loop: sim uses the component's real op-amp subckt (resolved from the symbol, not hardcoded), verify checks 1+Rf/Rg topologically (expected 10.000x / 20dB, simulated 9.995x -> 0.05% err, the real op-amp's finite-gain deviation). Second demo circuit exercises the loop on a different topology + active device. Built on okm.8.

Phase 0 complete: the full 6-stage loop (SKiDL -> parse -> validate -> simulate -> verify -> BOM) is proven end-to-end on two textbook circuits with known-good answers — RC low-pass (cutoff vs 1/2piRC, 0.001%) and non-inverting op-amp (gain vs 1+Rf/Rg, 0.05% via the component's real subckt model). Local-only, self-verifying, non-zero exit on failure. Stretch task P0.11 (layout scaffolding) superseded by the Layout epic's kicad-ipc-rs task (j54.5).

Dolt-backed parts library schema + read/write interface shipped. core::parts: parts/part_pins/part_ratings (mpn as natural key, citations via cited_page); PartsLibrary shells out to the dolt CLI (init + schema + transactional upsert + JSON reads + versioning commit), matching the project's shell-out pattern. PartRecord/PinRecord/RatingRecord, verified_by_human gate field, default cross-project dir (LOB_PARTS_DIR override). 'lob parts list/show/add/verify' CLI. Verified end-to-end (LM13700) + unit + real-dolt round-trip test. Unblocks okm.3 (fetch_datasheet), okm.7 (MPN resolution), okm.9 (SPICE models).

MPN resolution shipped. model::Part carries mpn (extracted from the netlist's MPN field); PartsLibrary::resolve_circuit returns per-part ResolutionStatus (NoMpn/Unknown/Unverified/Verified) against the library; 'lob parts resolve <circuit>'. Verified end-to-end: a SKiDL circuit with MPN=LM13700 resolves ⚠ unverified → ✓ verified. Unblocks zya.2 (Mouser pricing by resolved MPN) and okm.4 (verification gate). 41 core tests.

Mouser-priced BOM delivered end-to-end. zya.1: BomLine carries mpn+unit_price+ext_price, grouped by (mpn,value,footprint), Bom::total(), priced CSV+table. zya.2: core::mouser thin ureq client — search_mpn->PartPrice (matched MPN, stock, qty price breaks), unit_price_at, MOUSER_API_KEY from .env, response parsing unit-tested. zya.3: 'lob bom <circuit> --price --out <csv>'. Verified against LIVE Mouser API: LM13700M/NOPB priced .56 (1316 in stock) -> ext+total+CSV. 45 core tests.

Mouser-priced BOM delivered end-to-end. zya.1: BomLine carries mpn+unit_price+ext_price, grouped by (mpn,value,footprint), Bom::total(), priced CSV+table. zya.2: core::mouser thin ureq client — search_mpn->PartPrice (matched MPN, stock, qty price breaks), unit_price_at, MOUSER_API_KEY from .env, response parsing unit-tested. zya.3: 'lob bom <circuit> --price --out <csv>'. Verified against LIVE Mouser API: LM13700M/NOPB priced .56 (1316 in stock) -> ext+total+CSV. 45 core tests.

Mouser-priced BOM delivered end-to-end. zya.1: BomLine carries mpn+unit_price+ext_price, grouped by (mpn,value,footprint), Bom::total(), priced CSV+table. zya.2: core::mouser thin ureq client — search_mpn->PartPrice (matched MPN, stock, qty price breaks), unit_price_at, MOUSER_API_KEY from .env, response parsing unit-tested. zya.3: 'lob bom <circuit> --price --out <csv>'. Verified against LIVE Mouser API: LM13700M/NOPB priced .56 (1316 in stock) -> ext+total+CSV. 45 core tests.

fetch_datasheet delivered across two structured sources: KiCad official library (core::fetch::fetch_from_kicad — pins + datasheet URL) + JLCPCB distributor API (core::jlcpcb HMAC-signed, verified live — datasheet + structured ratings + MPN by LCSC code). Records keyed by MPN, unverified until confirmed; fetches MERGE across sources (CLI merge_fetched) so pins+ratings compose. PDF-page-citation extraction tier tracked as legion-of-bom-okm.11.

Distributor/CAD sourcing + trust order implemented: KiCad official CAD library + JLCPCB distributor API (per the DESIGN 3.5 trust order). Import-tooling directive (drive KiCad PCM / JLC2KICAD_lib per LIBRARIES.md) documented in notes. PDF fallback split to legion-of-bom-okm.11.

Verification gate shipped: PartResolution::blocks_verified_use + 'lob parts gate <circuit>' exits non-zero if any MPN-bearing part isn't human-verified — the enforceable structural block that layout/real-BOM-ordering call. Verified fail(exit1)->pass.

Human verification workflow: 'lob parts show <mpn>' presents extracted pins/ratings/datasheet + status; 'lob parts verify <mpn> --by' confirms ONCE per part (global, reused across projects). Tool-enforced version of the slew-limiter open-questions checklist.

Policy decided (LIBRARIES.md): a library's license is informational metadata surfaced to the user, NOT a pipeline gate — legion-of-bom doesn't police end-user usage. Only exposure is redistribution (CC-BY-SA 'collection' clause if we bundle library files). Verify a source before vouching (KiCad official confirmed CC-BY-SA 4.0). No license enforcement in the pipeline.

Board generation shipped: core::board::generate_board (netlist → valid .kicad_pcb, headless, direct S-expr). Loads .kicad_mod footprints, places (Placer trait — GridPlacer default, loop/PanelSpec placers plug in later) + net-wires them, 2-layer stack + net table. Deterministic UUIDs (sha2) → clean git diffs. core::sexpr rewritten as reader+writer (Sym/Str). 'lob board <circuit>'. Verified: kicad-cli reads it (DRC runs, nets wired) + exports full gerber set. Remaining board completeness is OTHER tasks: routing (j54.6 loop), ground pour (j54.2), board outline (PanelSpec j54.10). 54 core tests.

Board outline (Edge.Cuts rect from parts bbox + margin) + bottom-layer ground pour (B.Cu zone flooded to GND, DESIGN 6.2 default). Emitted via the generate_board seam; kicad-cli fills the zone on 'export gerbers --check-zones' (B.Cu gerber 459->965 bytes). Outline took DRC 1->0 violations. BoardOptions.ground_net/outline_margin_mm. Real panel-driven outline comes with PanelSpec (j54.10).
Two-layer obstacle-aware maze router (GridRouter) + Router seam; routes both demo circuits DRC-clean (0 violations, 0 unconnected), incl. vias on the RC ladder

Real TL072 flows through the whole pipeline (sim 20dB, verify gain 9.995x, BOM MPN, board routes DRC 0/0). Model travels with the part (Sim.* fields -> netlist -> resolver). Removed the is_opamp/is_ideal_opamp device-sniffing anti-pattern: analytic_check is now a registry of self-selecting checks. Added size-aware placement so real ICs don't collide.

Double-sided via explicit per-part Side declaration (default front). Back parts flip correctly (layers/coords/text), validated by render + DRC 0 errors. Mutable single-sided and Super Synthesis double-sided both handled.

Router now treats through-hole pads as free layer transitions (PadLayer::layers): a pad is a source/target on every layer it touches, so the router meets a through-hole pad on whichever side the net is already on instead of vias-ing onto its hole. double_sided.py hole_to_hole warnings 4->0; all boards 0 errors.

DRC readback via headless kicad-cli (not IPC — same de-risk as direct-gen). core::drc: run_drc shells kicad-cli pcb drc --format json --refill-zones, parses into DrcReport {violations/unconnected/parity} with severity helpers (errors/warnings/is_clean). CLI: lob drc <board> reports + exits non-zero on errors. This is the layout loop's check step (6.5 step 3). Surfaced a real latent issue (isolated GND pour -> j54.17 stitching).

lob fab: DRC-gated manufacturing package. core::fab exports Gerbers+drill (kicad-cli), auto-zips them for JLCPCB PCB upload, writes JLC-format CPL (Designator,Mid X,Mid Y,Layer,Rotation) reformatted from kicad pos, and JLC-format BOM (Comment,Designator,Footprint,LCSC) from generate_bom. Gated on run_drc (j54.11) — refuses to package a board with errors. Validated on opamp + double_sided: full gerber zip + CPL + BOM.
Done: aux_axis_origin at outline bottom-left; --use-drill-file-origin on pos+gerbers, --drill-origin plot on drill. Verified: opamp CPL R1 at (6.425, 7.205) instead of (100, -100).

lob guide: step-by-step visual assembly guide (HTML). core::guide parses the generated board for positions, groups parts into low-profile-first steps (resistors->diodes->connectors->switches->caps->transistors->ICs), renders a self-contained HTML page with a top-down SVG per step (current step's parts highlighted red, others greyed) + sorted parts list + polarity/pin-1 callouts + back-side notes. First cut = schematic top-down (accurate, no render dependency). Validated on opamp + double_sided.
Silk (refdes+polarity+pin-1) renders via wholesale footprint placement; added Reference-unhide for guaranteed visibility + 2 regression tests. Front on F.SilkS, back mirrored to B.SilkS. DRC clean.

DrcViolation::is_silkscreen_collision + DrcReport::silkscreen_collisions/count classify KiCad silk keys (silk_overlap/silk_over_copper) by 'silk' substring; CLI drc report surfaces them in a dedicated section with locations, separate from electrical warnings. Verified against a real colliding board (3 flagged) and clean boards (0). Not gated on is_clean (warnings, not electrical).

Photoreal unpopulated pcb render + analytic overlay mapping (f=0.70, centered, W/H from PNG header) shipped for HTML+PDF; markers upgraded to amber/red boxes + chip labels + polarity dots; multi-component grouping verified (R1+R2 in one step) and tested.

Photoreal unpopulated pcb render + analytic overlay mapping (f=0.70, centered, W/H from PNG header) shipped for HTML+PDF; markers upgraded to amber/red boxes + chip labels + polarity dots; multi-component grouping verified (R1+R2 in one step) and tested.

Photoreal unpopulated pcb render + analytic overlay mapping (f=0.70, centered, W/H from PNG header) shipped for HTML+PDF; markers upgraded to amber/red boxes + chip labels + polarity dots; multi-component grouping verified (R1+R2 in one step) and tested.

dqk.4: prep/sort sheet + per-step progress + BACK-side badges; assembly reordered per user feedback (type groups low->high, back side first, panel hardware last). dqk.5: back-side steps render on pcb render --side bottom with mirror_part_x so overlays land on real bottom pads (multi-image PDF).

dqk.4: prep/sort sheet + per-step progress + BACK-side badges; assembly reordered per user feedback (type groups low->high, back side first, panel hardware last). dqk.5: back-side steps render on pcb render --side bottom with mirror_part_x so overlays land on real bottom pads (multi-image PDF).

Guide upgraded from bare-minimum to photoreal unpopulated renders with accurate overlays (top+bottom), improved markers, verified multi-component grouping, a pull-and-sort prep sheet, per-step progress, and side-aware low-to-high assembly ordering. Deeper spatial access-aware sequencing spun out to j54.x (dqk.6).

Reconciled ~/src/puget-hardware/slew_limiter.py against slew-limiter-circuit.md: corrected LM13700 pinout (was wrong on every pin) from the cited KiCad symbol + datasheet, mono OTA-A topology, TL072 buffers, real QingPu PJ398SM jacks, Alpha pot, Eurorack 2x5 power header, decoupling. Parses to netlist; generates a board + photoreal 5-step guide (correct low->high ordering). Placeholders (C1, R3, R4 pulldown, rate range) flagged in-file.
Fixed: generate_board_report seeds each no-net pad as a single-pad obstacle net; GridRouter routes around them but never connects them. Slew limiter DRC 24 shorting_items -> 0. Regression test added.

Added rip-up-free retry to GridRouter: failed nets get grid priority on the next pass so their blockers reroute around them (route_pass + priority loop, <=6 rounds, deterministic). Slew limiter SIG_IN now routes: 1 unrouted -> 0. Existing routing/determinism tests unchanged. Fuller routing exposed a separate GND-pour-island bug (new bead).

Two-sided GND pour (THT pads bridge layers) + front-biased routing (back_penalty_mm keeps signals off the pour layer) -> ground plane stays intact, no more isolated-island starved_thermal. Slew limiter fully DRC-clean (0 errors, 0 unconnected). Example boards unaffected. Tests added.

Transient sim capability delivered: TranAnalysis + generate_tran_deck (PWL step) + simulate_tran + slew/rise metrics, shared body-emission with the AC path. Validated end-to-end on an RC step response (ngspice). Slew-limiter values (R3=47k, C1=47nF) confirmed via a datasheet-behavioural OTA core: 10.23 V/ms = IABC/C1, 10V in ~1ms. Full LM13700-part pipeline run still needs the cited SPICE model (okm.9).

EurorackPlacer: panel-facing parts anchored at their panel-cutout positions (via optional refdes on cutouts), rest spread down the tall board by first-fit collision placement; courtyard_extent spaces by real courtyards; BoardOptions.fixed_outline + lob board --panel. Slew limiter (8HP) now generates a vertical 3U module, 0 DRC errors, matching the panel PCB. Non-panel boards unchanged.

Net.net_class on the model + parser reads (class ...) from the netlist; is_critical() queryable via CircuitSource. Carrier is SKiDL's existing net-class field (net.netclass = NetClass("Critical") -> (class "Critical")), no sidecar. Unit-tested (critical_net_class_survives_parse).

LayoutMode {Analog,Digital,Mixed} + CostWeights + score() in layout.rs; --mode on lob board/fab. Analog weights critical-net + signal wirelength, Digital vias+length. Unit-tested (score monotonicity, analog>digital on criticals).

Delivered on the EXISTING anchored placement per agreed scope; the j54.1/j54.10 PanelProfile refactor was deliberately deferred (still open, tracked separately) — not a blocker for the working loop. run_layout_loop: place(SeededPlacer, connectivity-seeded) -> route -> in-process check -> repair -> keep best; surfaces unresolved+critical nets (6.8); optional DRC gate. Real slew board: total HPWL -34%, DRC-clean via lob fab. Fixed a real courtyard-measurement bug (fp_circle radius, pin-1 origin offset) underlying placement collision-avoidance.

Tagged SLEW_NODE (high-Z OTA feedback), SIG_IN + SIG_OUT (panel audio I/O) via a critical() helper (net.netclasses=NetClass('Critical')) in slew_limiter.py. Verified end-to-end: netlist emits (class 'Default,Critical'); is_critical() now parses comma-joined classes; analog loop reports critical 53mm and drives SLEW_NODE 86->8.6mm, SIG_IN 32->14mm. DRC-clean. Fab regenerated.
footprint_shape: Thonkiconn/PJ301 now resolve to a 6mm barrel Circle (JACK_BARREL_MM), not the body rect — the nut can tighten. Also fixes the hand-authored slew panel.
A* landed: route_one uses an admissible Manhattan heuristic; 8HP gen 110s+->6.6s, routing still optimal (route tests pass, clippy/fmt clean).

Rip-up-and-reroute landed: route_pass reports blame (nets whose committed copper boxes in an unreachable pad); route() forces those blockers to route after the victim via accumulating ordering constraints + topo-sort (cycles dropped). Combined with A* + a conservative rotation heuristic (only rotate parts too wide for the board), the CV-added SMD PCBA slew limiter now routes 6HP AND 8HP fully DRC-clean (0 errors, was 10-13). 4HP 103->46 (still architectural, see 3jh). 128 tests pass.

Router insets copper by edge_clearance_mm (0.5) + widest-copper-half inside the board outline; board.rs passes the panel outline as route bounds. 8HP slew limiter stays 0 DRC errors; 6HP track copper_edge_clearance errors eliminated (remaining 6HP errors are J4 placement-at-edge, filed separately). Regression test routing_refuses_to_enter_the_board_edge_band; 130 tests, clippy/fmt clean.

overflow_drop() stacks unfittable free parts in an off-board lane 2mm below the bottom edge instead of straddling it at y1+2 (~1mm inside), so an overflowed part no longer trips copper_edge_clearance — the failure surfaces honestly as unrouted nets. Fixed in both SeededPlacer and EurorackPlacer. 8HP slew limiter unchanged (0 DRC errors); 6HP CV jack J4 drops 3.9mm below the edge, 3 nets unconnected (J4 unfittable on 30mm = 3jh, not this bug). Regression test overflow_parts_drop_clear_below_the_board_edge; 131 tests, clippy/fmt clean.

subboard.rs generates KiCad .kicad_mod footprints from a spec (rows/pins/pitch/spacing/body); THT header pads reach both layers, courtyard=body=keep-out, pin-1 marked. Built-in Daisy Seed (18x51mm, 2x20 @2.54mm, DIP numbering). load_footprint synthesizes the reserved 'LobModule:' lib in-memory, so a part with footprint 'LobModule:Daisy_Seed' places+routes through the normal pipeline with no file on disk. Validated end-to-end through real KiCad: daisy_demo (Daisy + 2 SMD passives) places, routes 14 tracks 0 unconnected; only DRC error is a GND-pour thermal island (filed 25z.2, inherent to dense THT grids), not a footprint defect. 135 tests (4 new), clippy/fmt clean.

ground_stitching_vias places a GND via just off each through-hole ground pad, tying the F.Cu/B.Cu pours together locally so a pour island fenced off by a dense THT header grid reconnects to the intact pour on the other layer. Placed inside the board edge and clear of all other-net copper (pads/tracks/vias); a boxed-in pad is skipped, not forced. Daisy demo: 1 starved_thermal -> 0 DRC errors (1 stitch added). 8HP slew limiter unchanged: 0 errors (7 stitches added, improves the ground plane). 2 unit tests + 137 total, clippy/fmt clean.

SubboardSpec gains per-pad PinLabel (name + aliases); daisy_seed() carries the real Electrosmith pinout (libDaisy Daisy_Seed_Rev4_Pinout.csv) — D0-D30, AUDIO_IN/OUT_L/R, AGND/3V3_ANA/3V3_DIG/VIN/GND, with A0-A11 ADC aliases; the DIP pad numbering already matched the physical layout. pad_for(name)/pin_name(pad) lookups (case-insensitive + aliases); function-name labels emitted on F.Fab. board.rs resolves a net to a pad by pad number OR function name, so a net wired to 'AUDIO_OUT_L' connects pad 18. Daisy demo stays 0 DRC errors with fab labels. 140 tests (5 new), clippy/fmt clean.

Height/collision check (DESIGN 6.7): part_height_mm heuristic by footprint family + SubboardSpec.standoff_mm (Daisy 11mm); detect_collisions flags same-side parts whose keep-out overlaps a sub-board body and exceed its standoff. Wired through BoardArtifacts.collisions + LayoutReport.collisions + findings; lob board prints 'mechanical clearance issue(s)'. Unit-tested (tall-under vs short/back/clear-of). Daisy demo + slew limiter: 0 false collisions, still DRC-clean. NOTE: with the current placer keeping parts out of the sub-board courtyard, this is a safety net; it fires once a placer packs under sub-boards (filed follow-up). 142 tests (2 new), clippy/fmt clean.

Real, general packing under stacked sub-boards. (1) Region-based side+THT-aware placement: PartFacts now carries height_mm, standoff_mm, and tht_pads (through-hole pad keep-outs); placement_clear enforces — bodies clash only same-side and only when neither passes under the other (standoff vs height); through-hole pins are both-side hard keep-outs every body avoids; a candidate's own pins may pass over a standoff but not into a surface part. (2) Sub-board courtyard = per-header-row rects (general from spec.rows), not the full body, so KiCad stops false-flagging same-side parts in the open centre. Nothing Daisy-hardcoded (driven by pad layers + standoff + rows). Verified real KiCad: 8HP slew limiter 0 errors (side-aware placement, no regression); daisy_demo 0 errors; daisy_panel_demo 0 errors with 6/12 support parts packed UNDER the Daisy in the clear centre (was 25 errors with naive packing). Tall parts kept out (height gate); 25z.4 collision check remains the backstop. 144 tests (placement_clear rules + pack-under/tall-out).
